activation: Tap the assistant icon on the center display
compatibility: All Gen 1 and Gen 2 R1S and R1T owners. Expected to be available on R2 at launch.
alexa: Enabling Rivian Assistant disables Alexa integration — you cannot run both simultaneously.
ownerNote: I've been using Hey Rivian since 2026.15 dropped. Sometimes it's great, sometimes it's not.  The biggest thing: you don't need to memorize commands — just talk to it like a person and it figures it out. In my experience, conversational navigation chaining feels noticeably better than Alexa.

limitations: Requires active Connect+ subscription or trial ($14.99/month or $149.99/year)
Many assistant features require an internet connection — some local vehicle controls may still function without connectivity
Some owners report unintentional wake word activations at highway speed
Google Calendar is the primary third-party integration available at launch — Apple Calendar and Outlook support coming in future updates
tips: Use conversational phrasing rather than keyword commands. 'I'm cold' works better than 'Set HVAC 70'.
The assistant appears to maintain context during a session — you can say 'make it warmer' after setting a temperature without repeating yourself.
Connect Google Calendar in Settings for meeting navigation and related assistant features.
Some preferences and context may be tied to the active driver profile — household members may not see your settings.
You can disable the Hey Rivian wake word, limit location sharing, or turn off the memory feature in privacy settings.

faq.question: Does Hey Rivian require Connect+?
faq.answer: Yes. Hey Rivian requires an active Connect+ subscription ($14.99/month or $149.99/year). Some local vehicle controls may work without connectivity, but most assistant features require both Connect+ and an internet connection.

faq.question: Can I use Hey Rivian and Alexa at the same time?
faq.answer: No. Enabling Rivian Assistant disables Alexa integration — you cannot run both simultaneously. You'll need to choose one.

faq.question: What Rivian vehicles support Hey Rivian?
faq.answer: All Gen 1 and Gen 2 R1S and R1T owners on software 2026.15 or later. Hey Rivian is expected to be available on R2 at launch.

faq.question: What software version do I need?
faq.answer: Hey Rivian requires software 2026.15 or later. Check your version under Settings → About → Software Version. Updates can be installed via the Rivian app or in-vehicle Settings.

faq.question: How do I activate Hey Rivian?
faq.answer: Three ways: say 'Hey Rivian' or 'OK Rivian', hold the left steering wheel button, or tap the assistant icon on the center display.

faq.question: Does Hey Rivian work without cell service?
faq.answer: Partially. Some local vehicle controls (like adjusting climate or suspension) may still function without connectivity. Navigation, general knowledge, and calendar features require an active internet connection.

faq.question: Can Hey Rivian read and send text messages?
faq.answer: Yes. You can say 'Read my messages' or 'Text Sarah and tell her I'm running late.' Natural phrasing works — you don't need to dictate the exact message word for word.

faq.question: Does Hey Rivian integrate with Google Calendar?
faq.answer: Yes. Connect Google Calendar in Settings to unlock meeting navigation, departure time estimates, and appointment lookups. Apple Calendar and Outlook support are expected in a future update.

faq.question: Can Hey Rivian answer questions about my vehicle?
faq.answer: Yes. You can ask things like 'What does this warning light mean?' or 'What tire pressure should I use?' Rivian describes the assistant as a built-in encyclopedia for your vehicle.

faq.question: Do I need to memorize exact commands?
faq.answer: No. Hey Rivian is designed for conversational phrasing. 'I'm cold' works just as well as 'Set cabin temperature to 70 degrees.' Speaking naturally tends to get better results than keyword-style commands.

changes: Initial launch — Hey Rivian available to all Gen 1 and Gen 2 R1S and R1T owners on Connect+
Google Calendar integration — first third-party productivity integration
Full vehicle control, navigation chaining, media, messaging, and owner's guide categories live
